What common template you mean Pawel?
I've changed it to cmpv2_enabled (removed ".enabled" part). Is it fine for you Sylvain?

Hi Sylvain,
could you explain to me how these common templates work?
We just include common template and override chosen values using values.yaml?
If so, is there any documentation which values I can override?

Yes, just include common template (like you did for common.fullname").

For the service part, it relies on a well structured "service" key in values.yaml
kubernetes/common/common folder has a documentation.rst almost up to date with (almost maybe now) all the current available templates.
In kubernetes/common/common/templates, you'll see their implementation and (most of the time again...) their usage

but again, https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/103421 is a good example of "real life" use

No, I didn't check this. Is it possible to set less than 1 cpu?
The Java and Spring require some memory. 1Gi should be enough.
ok 1G of RAM, for CPU, let's say you need on average (that's what request is) 10% of a CPU, then you can put cpu: 100m (100 milli)

Hi Sylvain,
Do you have an example of how to use the common templates for deployment.yaml etc. when there are several deployments within a file that require different values, as in this file, and similarly for the service.yaml for aaf-ejbca?

Hi Emmet,

Sorry I didn't saw you have 2 deployment / services in the same file.
We (in ONAP) usually use a subchart for database so we don't really have the issue...


If you don't need 10.2 specifically but 10.1 is fine, you can (easily) use our common galera instance or the common chart (NBI for instance is using it)

Hi Sylvain,
Apologies, but could you point me towards an example of how to use the common-galera instance or common chart that I could use for configuring my services and deployment file? It's been a while since I've done helm stuff, I think I need to see a working example to understand how it would work.

Hi Emmet, no need to apologize, this is a perfectly fine request :)
I think the simplest example is NBI : https://github.com/onap/oom/tree/master/kubernetes/nbi


Basically:
Either you use your own mariadb galera instance (if global.mariadbGalera.localcluster is set to true) or you use the common one (global.mariadbGalera.localcluster set to false).


Then look at 
 * https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/nbi/values.yaml#L25 to 30 : this is the global "configuration" of global mariadb instance (service name, port, ...). 
   You should copy paste into your own values.yaml

 * https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/nbi/values.yaml#L35 to 41: this is to create the relevant secrets for your user in the db and for the root user 
   Copy paste and change nbi by ejbca for example

 * https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/nbi/values.yaml#L63 to 66: this is where we set the username and the password (here it's commented out, meaning that a password is generated but you can retrieve it of course with the secret).
   Again, copy/paste with relevant user name 

 * https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/nbi/values.yaml#L68 to 82 is the configuration when you want a local cluster. 
   Idem, copy/paste and change everything with "nbi" by something meaningful for your component.

 * https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/nbi/values.yaml#L84 to 86: this is the specific configuration for your component in the global mariadb galera (roughly, it will create your user and the database and after it's up to you) 


Now you have to add the requirements in requirements.yaml (just copy/paste here)

You'll have to create a "secret file" (copy paste the one from nbi but user "common.secretFast" which is ... faster.

Last stuff is to give the right value to your actual deployment.

https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/nbi/templates/deployment.yaml#L56 to 57 is the stuff and you can copy/paste as it's pure templating.

https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/nbi/templates/deployment.yaml#L58 and https://github.com/onap/oom/blob/master/kubernetes/nbi/templates/deployment.yaml#L60 will show you how to give username and password (copy paste but change the nbi part to reflect what you did in values.yaml)


And you should be good to go!

Hope I'm clear

@Sylvain and @Krzysztof: I would like to keep this file as secret without ENV substitution. What we can change is to unhardcode iak/rv and use helm variables to substitute them - both here and in EJBCA (as this configuration must match).

Other file (default/cmpServer.json) is meant to be filled in during deployment time if operator would like to use certificates from CMPv2 server and will have to provide similar configuration there which will point to its CMPv2 server and we can't guarantee correct ENV substitution in such case. This file may contain from 0-N configured CAs, for testing purpose it contains just 2.

Sylvain asked us to enable it for testing purposes.

@Sylvain
To your question from the comment:
Just use the same trick as we do for deployer creds in aaf init container:
 externalSecret: '{{ ternary (tpl (default "" .Values.credentials.cmp.clientIakExternalSecret) .) "cmpv2TestingDisabled" .Values.global.cmpv2Testing }}

This will prevent secrets from being created if cmpv2Testing is set to false;)

Yes yes I know how nasty it is but think also how powerful:D
